A Peloton is a rideshare service where riders can sign up and ride with virtual athletes.  The service was founded in 2012 by Sam Altman and Travis Scott.  The Peloton uses a cadence as a method of measuring distance and time to be able to compare to a live athlete.  The cadence is designed to be broken down into power and speed segments.  The power segment is measured in terms of time taken to complete a full lap around a 360 degree circle.  Speed is measured in terms of how fast an athlete is able to run, stand, and bike while maintaining a constant cadence.
